mission.title: logging-operator-kube-logging-: Ability to select namespaces by labels
mission.description: Ability to select namespaces by labels. Requested by 2+ users.

steps.title: Check current logging-operator-kube-logging- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your logging-operator-kube-logging- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n logging-operator-kube-logging- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=logging-operator-kube-logging-
helm list -n logging-operator-kube-logging- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working logging-operator-kube-logging- installation.

steps.title: Review logging-operator-kube-logging- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant logging-operator-kube-logging- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n logging-operator-kube-logging- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=logging-operator-kube-logging-
kubectl get configmap -n logging-operator-kube-logging- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=logging-operator-kube-logging-
```
We'd like to be able to define a ClusterFlow that defines an entire subset of namespaces which are labeled. This would allow teams to have a single ClusterFlow instead of potential hundreds of Flows.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ability to select namespaces by labels
steps.description:
Allow routing based on namespace labels if the feature is enabled in Fluent Bit, see: https://kube-logging.dev/4.6/docs/whats-new/#kubernetes-namespace-labels-and-annotations

Configure Fluent Bit in the Logging resource or separately like this:
```
kind: Logging
spec:
  fluentbit:
   
```yaml
kind: Logging
spec:
  fluentbit:
    filterKubernetes:
      namespace_labels: "On"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n logging-operator-kube-logging- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=logging-operator-kube-logging-
kubectl get events -n logging-operator-kube-logging- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ability to select namespaces by labels" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Allow routing based on namespace labels if the feature is enabled in Fluent Bit, see: https://kube-logging.dev/4.6/docs/whats-new/#kubernetes-namespace-labels-and-annotations

Configure Fluent Bit in the Logging resource or separately like this:
```
kind: Logging
spec:
  fluentbit:
    filterKubernetes:
      namespace_labels: "On"
```

or

```
kind: FluentbitAgent
spec:
  filterKubernetes:
codeSnippets:
kind: Logging
spec:
  fluentbit:
    filterKubernetes:
      namespace_labels: "On"

apiVersion: logging.banzaicloud.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterFlow
metadata:
  name: sample
spec:
  match:
    - select:
        namespace_labels:
          tenant: devs
  gblobalOutputRefs:
    - example

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with logging-operator-kube-logging- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
